growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: silva.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Suzuki et al. Bacterial genome reduction using the progressive clustering of deletions via yeast sexual cycling. Genome Research (2015). DOI:10.1101/gr.182477.114. [PMC4352883]
growth_records.snippet: the seven-deletion strain was 63 ± 2 min, which is comparable to the doubling times of the JCVI-syn1.0 and JCVI-syn1.0 Δ1–6 strains (63 ± 4 min and 59 ± 2 min, respectively)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (63 +/- 4 min = 1.05 h) explicitly reported (measured by quantifying cell-associated DNA increase with PicoGreen during log-phase static liquid culture at 37C in SP-4+17% FBS; doubling time = ln2/exponential rate). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/1.05 = 0.66 h^-1. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.cite: Suzuki et al. Bacterial genome reduction using the progressive clustering of deletions via yeast sexual cycling. Genome Research (2015). DOI:10.1101/gr.182477.114. [PMC4352883]
growth_records.snippet: ...comparable to the doubling times of the JCVI-syn1.0 and JCVI-syn1.0 Δ1–6 strains (63 ± 4 min and 59 ± 2 min, respectively)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (59 +/- 2 min = 0.9833 h) explicitly reported (same DNA-quantification growth assay as JCVI-syn1.0 above, SP-4+17% FBS, 37C static culture). mu = ln2/0.9833 = 0.7049 h^-1. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.strain: eight-deletion strain (derived from JCVI-syn1.0 Δ1-6, 8 additional gene clusters deleted, 91 genes/~10% genome)
growth_records.cite: Suzuki et al. Bacterial genome reduction using the progressive clustering of deletions via yeast sexual cycling. Genome Research (2015). DOI:10.1101/gr.182477.114. [PMC4352883]
growth_records.snippet: the eight-deletion strain was slow-growing with a doubling time of 113 ± 5 min (n = 3)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (113 +/- 5 min = 1.8833 h) explicitly reported via DNA-quantification growth assay (SP-4+17% FBS, 37C static culture). mu = ln2/1.8833 = 0.368 h^-1. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.strain: single-deletion mutant lacking gene cluster MMSYN1_0840-0846 (the 'slow' deletion, an oligopeptide ABC transporter cluster)
growth_records.cite: Suzuki et al. Bacterial genome reduction using the progressive clustering of deletions via yeast sexual cycling. Genome Research (2015). DOI:10.1101/gr.182477.114. [PMC4352883]
growth_records.snippet: it was not slower than one of the single mutant strains with a doubling time of 138 ± 2 min
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (138 +/- 2 min = 2.3 h) explicitly reported for the single-mutant strain carrying the MMSYN1_0840-0846 deletion, identified as the slow-growth-causing deletion and therefore excluded from the final 7-deletion strain construction. mu = ln2/2.3 = 0.3013 h^-1. Same DNA-quantification assay, SP-4+17% FBS, 37C. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.strain: single-deletion mutants carrying one of the other seven deletion-cluster genotypes (pooled range across 7 distinct single mutants)
growth_records.cite: Suzuki et al. Bacterial genome reduction using the progressive clustering of deletions via yeast sexual cycling. Genome Research (2015). DOI:10.1101/gr.182477.114. [PMC4352883]
growth_records.snippet: ...to those of single mutants carrying one of the seven deletions (68.3–76 min)
growth_records.notes: Doubling times explicitly reported as a range of 68.3-76 min (1.1383-1.2667 h) across the seven distinct single-deletion mutant strains (each lacking one of the 7 clusters retained in the final multi-deletion strain). This corresponds to mu range 0.5472-0.6089 h^-1; growth_rate_per_h/doubling_time_h fields report the approximate midpoint of this range (0.578 h^-1 / 1.2 h) since this is an aggregate of multiple distinct strains rather than one single strain. Confidence medium because this single record aggregates several genetically distinct strains rather than one specific condition. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]
